page: Amazon Web Services x8i.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing the Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache configuration includes 48 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 480 MB L3 cache. The CPU supports Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). This instance does not include local storage or GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory and processing capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core, image processing, and text processing tests. It also records top-tier results in LLM inference speeds for prompt processing and text generation using a small 135M model. Memory bandwidth for cached small blocks is strong, placing in the top 25%, while uncached large block read and write operations perform in the average tier. Multi-core processing, software compilation, and database operations consistently rank in the top 25% across Passmark and Geekbench suites.

From a resource density perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU acceleration. However, the lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads. The instance is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, Redis deployments, and CPU-based machine learning inference.

Benchmark results show top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core, image processing, and text processing, alongside strong multi-core and database operation metrics. It is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring 768 GB of DDR5 RAM and Intel Xeon processors for memory-intensive database workloads.

768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 16.0 GB per core
